title: Can you enforce strong Active Directory password rules without frustrating users?
summary: Strong Active Directory passwords don't have to come at the expense of usability. Specops Software explains how passphrases, breached password protection, and self-service resets can improve security without frustrating users. [...]

title: Glassworm botnet disrupted after resilient C2 infrastructure takedown
summary: The Glassworm botnet targeting developers in software supply-chain attacks has been disrupted after researchers took down its resilient command-and-control infrastructure relying on Solana blockchain transactions and the BitTorrent DHT network. [...]

title: FBI warns of in-person data theft attacks from extortion gang
summary: The FBI warned on Tuesday that the Silent Ransom Group (SRG) extortion gang is now targeting U.S.-based law firms in in-person data theft attacks. [...]

title: CISA gives feds 4 days to patch actively exploited cPanel plugin flaw
summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has given U.S. federal agencies four days to secure their servers against a critical vulnerability in the LiteSpeed cPanel user-end plugin, which is actively being exploited in attacks. [...]

title: Dutch police arrests suspect linked to Ajax football club hack
summary: The Dutch National Police arrested a 35-year-old man suspected of hacking the professional football club Ajax Amsterdam (AFC Ajax) earlier this year. [...]

title: Windows 11 KB5089573 update released with performance improvements
summary: Microsoft has released the KB5089573 preview cumulative update for Windows 11 versions 25H2 and 24H2, which comes with 30 changes, including performance and reliability improvements. [...]

title: KnowledgeDeliver flaw exploited as a zero-day to install web shells
summary: Hackers exploited a critical zero-day vulnerability in a server running the KnowledgeDeliver learning management system (LMS) to deploy the Godzilla web shell. [...]

title: Charter confirms data breach after ShinyHunters extortion threat
summary: U.S. telecommunications giant Charter Communications has confirmed it suffered a data breach after the ShinyHunters extortion group threatened to leak stolen data unless a ransom is paid. [...]

title: How Varonis Atlas integrates Claude Compliance API for AI governance
summary: AI governance requires visibility into how AI tools interact with enterprise data. Varonis explains how its Atlas platform uses Claude Compliance API data to help monitor usage, investigate risk, and support compliance. [...]

title: Microsoft Defender can now automatically isolate hacked endpoints
summary: Microsoft is testing a new Defender for Endpoint capability that will automatically isolate compromised endpoints to thwart attackers' attempts to move laterally across the network. [...]

title: Webinar: Too many tools are slowing network incident response
summary: IT teams often need to jump between monitoring dashboards, infrastructure tools, ticketing systems, and communication platforms during network incidents. This webinar explores how automation and AI-assisted workflows can help reduce manual coordination and improve incident response times. [...]

title: CISA orders feds to patch actively exploited Drupal vulnerability
summary: CISA has given U.S. government agencies until Wednesday evening to secure their servers against an SQL injection vulnerability in the Drupal content management system (CMS) that it flagged as actively exploited. [...]

title: Microsoft: Domain Controller lookup may fail on Windows Server 2016
summary: Microsoft has confirmed a new known issue affecting Windows Server 2016 systems that causes domain controller lookups to fail after installing the KB5087537 May 2026 security update. [...]

title: 7-Eleven data breach exposes personal information of 185,000 people
summary: The ShinyHunters extortion gang stole the personal information of over 183,000 people after hacking the systems of convenience store chain giant 7-Eleven in April, according to data breach notification service Have I Been Pwned. [...]

title: Anthropic’s restricted Claude Mythos model may be coming to Claude Code
summary: Anthropic appears to be preparing for the public rollout of the Mythos model, which was announced in April as a restricted model that poses major security risks to private and public software. [...]
